channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Greek singer-songwriter Dionysis Savvopoulos buried in a state funeral
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Popular Greek singer-songwriter Dionysis Savvopoulos was buried Saturday at Athens’ First Cemetery in a state-sponsored funeral, four days after his death at age 80. Savvopoulos had died of a heart attack after battling cancer since 2020.
item.dc:creator: DEMETRIS NELLAS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Company recalls 2 million pounds of barbecue pork jerky that may contain metal wire
item.description: (The Associated Press) -- A South Dakota company is recalling more than 2.2 million pounds (998,000 kilograms) of Korean barbecue pork jerky sold at Costco and Sam's Club stores because the product may be contaminated with pieces of metal, federal health officials said Friday. LSI, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Jonel Aleccia, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 2 dead, several critically injured after 13 shot at large Halloween party in Robeson County, deputies say
item.description: ROBESON COUNTY, N.C. (WBTW) -- Two people were killed and several others critically injured overnight when 13 people were shot at a large party in Robeson County, deputies said. It happened before 3 a.m. Saturday in the area of Dixon Road and McGirt Gin Road outside of Maxton.
item.dc:creator: Madi Codispoti, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A bomb in Gaza's rubble wounds twins who thought it was a toy
item.description: GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip (AP) — The Shorbasi family was sitting in their severely damaged house in Gaza City, enjoying the relative calm of the ceasefire. Then they heard an explosion and rushed outside to find their 6-year-old twins bleeding on the ground.
item.dc:creator: ABDEL KAREEM HANA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How the growing penny shortage may impact you
item.description: Several retailers have started to roll out policies as they run out of pennies. Here's what to expect if you're paying with cash.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: By the Numbers: Why trick-or-treaters may bag more gummy candy than chocolate this Halloween
item.description: Trick-or-treaters may find more fruity candy than chocolate among their Halloween handouts this year.
item.dc:creator: Dee-Ann Durbin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Historic libraries bring modern comfort to book lovers and history buffs in New England
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When David Arsenault takes down a worn, leather-bound 19th-century book from the winding shelves of the Boston Athenaeum, he feels a sense of awe — like he’s handling an artifact in a museum.
item.dc:creator: LEAH WILLINGHAM and KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'ghost tapping?' New scheme targeting tap-to-pay users
item.description: The Better Business Bureau is warning tap-to-pay users of a new scheme that could make simple transactions costly.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, KREX 5

item.title: Thailand's Queen Mother Sirikit has died at age 93
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Thailand’s Queen Mother Sirikit, who supervised royal projects to help the rural poor, preserve traditional craft-making and protect the environment, died on Friday. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: DENIS D. GRAY, JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI and TIAN MACLEOD JI, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Grandmothers in Colombia get the quinceañera they never had
item.description: BOGOTÁ, Colombia (AP) — At age 72, Rosalba Casas finally celebrated her 15th birthday on Friday, donning a flowing pink dress and a tiara for the quinceañera she dreamed of as a teenager.
item.dc:creator: ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Russia faces a shrinking and aging population and tries restrictive laws to combat it
item.description: For a quarter century, President Vladimir Putin has faced the specter of Russia's shrinking and aging population. In 1999, a year before he came to power, the number of babies born in Russia plunged to its lowest recorded level.
item.dc:creator: KATIE MARIE DAVIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Photos of Melodee Buzzard 'look like her': Aunt
item.description: Melodee's aunt, Lizabeth Meza, joined "Banfield" on Friday night and said she had not seen Melodee for over four years.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Anti-abortion pregnancy centers are looking to offer much more than ultrasounds and diapers
item.description: More anti-abortion pregnancy centers have been offering medical services such as testing for sexually transmitted infections.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Mulvihill And Kimberlee Kruesi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bill to fund SNAP in shutdown introduced in House
item.description: Rep. Mariannette Miller-Meeks (R-Iowa) on Friday introduced a bill to fund the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP) as the government shutdown threatens payments of the food aid in some states.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In emotional speech, Zohran Mamdani defends Muslim identity against 'racist and baseless' attacks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Zohran Mamdani, the Democratic nominee for New York City mayor, pledged Friday to further embrace his Muslim identity in response to growing attacks by former Gov. Andrew Cuomo and his surrogates that he characterized as “racist and baseless.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Chicago student journalists create map of ICE sightings
item.description: Loyola University's student newspaper's map of confirmed ICE sightings serves a larger purpose in a diverse neighborhood
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: US says it now plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia as soon as Oct. 31
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — The U.S. government plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, and could do so as early as Oct. 31, according to a Friday court filing.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's trade war with Canada: A timeline of how we got here
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's barrage of tariffs has quickly strained the United States' longstanding relationship with Canada over the last year. Since taking office in January, Trump has threatened Canada's economy and sovereignty with steep import taxes on its goods.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon sends largest aircraft carrier to Caribbean amid boat strikes
item.description: Hegseth’s order comes as the Trump administration has been striking alleged drug-smuggling boats in the Caribbean Sea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Task force says man to be deported 3rd time after arrest
item.description: The task force said he has an active order of removal from the country and will be deported for the third time.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration seeks to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia
item.description: The Justice Department floated sending Kilmar Abrego Garcia to Liberia, marking the administration's latest effort to remove the once-deported man amid its ongoing prosecution of him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Cholesterol medication recalled over 'failed dissolution specifications': What to know
item.description: “In some instances, stopping your medicine may be more harmful to your health than continuing to take the recalled medicine,” the FDA says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Jeffries endorses Mamdani in New York City mayor's race
item.description: Just hours before the start of New York’s early voting, Jeffries broke that silence by endorsing the controversial candidate.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Judge halts death penalty case in New Mexico due to federal shutdown
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — A judge in New Mexico has halted a federal death penalty case, citing the pinch of the U.S. government shutdown and a funding shortfall for specialized public defenders that stretches back to the summer.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California wildlife officers euthanize several gray wolves after attacks on livestock
item.description: “This decision was not made lightly nor was it easy,” said Charlton H. Bonham, director of California’s Department of Fish and Wildlife.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, NewsNation

item.title: How Hispanics' views of Trump have changed since January, according to a new AP-NORC poll
item.description: President Donald Trump's favorability has fallen among Hispanic adults since the beginning of the year, a new AP-NORC poll shows, a potential warning sign from a key constituency that helped secure his victory in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Illinois gubernatorial candidate's son, daughter-in-law and grandchildren killed in helicopter crash
item.description: Darren Bailey, a former state senator, officially kicked off his campaign last month to regain the Republican nomination for governor after losing to Democratic Gov. JB Pritzker in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Winnie Dortch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SNAP benefits may run out Nov. 1. Here's how you could still get food assistance
item.description: A disruption or delay could impact 42 million people, or 1 in 8 Americans, who rely on SNAP to buy food.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How Trump and Xi are doing a delicate, sometimes pointed dance in bilateral trade talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The calm following the Sept. 19 phone call between U.S. President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping had barely ended when strong undercurrents surged, threatening to rock the entire ship. First, the U.S. government widened sanction rules on Chinese companies.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How inflatable costumes ballooned at anti-Trump rallies due to a protester's frog outfit
item.description: Puffed-out costumes — hippos in tutus, Mr. Potato Heads, dinosaurs — have quickly become a feature of protests against President Donald Trump’s administration, including the massive "No Kings" marches across the U.S. last weekend.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 family members of Illinois governor candidate killed in Montana helicopter crash, campaign says
item.description: Four family members of a Republican running for governor in Illinois were killed in a Montana helicopter crash, his campaign said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cracker Barrel CEO explains reason for attempt to change logo: report
item.description: Cracker Barrel had debuted its new logo in August, but quickly faced backlash for ditching a depiction of the “Old Timer” previously seen on the sign.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Where the jewels stolen from the Louvre Museum might end up
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Just days after a stunning heist at the Louvre Museum in Paris, speculation is growing around where the lavish, stolen jewels that once adorned France's royals might end up.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and R.J. RICO, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Medicare agency recalling thousands of furloughed staffers for open enrollment
item.description: The Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) is temporarily calling back all furloughed staff starting next week, a spokesperson confirmed Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: White House releases donor list for Trump ballroom amid East Wing demolition
item.description: Amazon, Apple, Google and other major companies are among those donating to help cover the cost of President Trump's massive new ballroom at the White House. The White House confirmed the list to The Hill amid demolition of the East Wing to make room for the ballroom.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: Philadelphia police find human remains in search for missing woman
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Philadelphia police found human remains Saturday in their search for a 23-year-old woman who disappeared from her nursing home job about two weeks ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4
